finding: classifyImageSrc('/\evil.test') returns 'allowed' because the !startsWith('//') guard only rejects a literal double-slash; the URL parser treats backslash as slash under http, resolving /\evil.test to https://evil.test — a model-emitted renders an exfil beacon to an attacker origin.

hasMarkdownTable/Image/CodeFence regex-scan the FULL uncapped block text on every call; estimateSize is invoked by getMeasurements for each unmeasured index and re-runs on every measurement (itemSizeCacheVersion bump, pendingMin collapse on upward scroll) → O(n×textlen) repeated ~n times, replacing the old O(1) constant.

estimateSize calls contentWidth() → getScrollElement().clientWidth; getMeasurements runs in the render triggered by _measureElement (which just wrote layout/scrollTop), so the clientWidth read forces a synchronous reflow — one forced layout per measurement event during scroll, in the hot path.

onChange's `if(!sync) recordMeasurements(itemSizeCache)` iterates the ENTIRE itemSizeCache (delete+set per entry) on every notify(false); notify(false) fires per row measurement during scroll, not only at scroll-end, so a scroll-through costs 1+2+…+n = O(n²) Map ops.

initialMeasurementsCache is memoized on [messagesArray] and first built when getScrollElement() is still null → contentWidth() falls back (wrong width bucket); react-virtual consumes the seed once, while onChange/unmount write at the REAL bucket, so on a narrow pane the warm-reopen seed never matches the write bucket and ITEM-2 silently degrades to the estimator.

The unmount-flush cleanup is a passive effect running after the scroll DOM detaches; getScrollElement().clientWidth is then 0 → contentWidth() = -32 → widthBucket 0, recording every measured height under garbage bucket 0 that no future open reads, defeating the flush and polluting the LRU.

setMeasuredHeight's changed-value branch does store.set(key,size) with no preceding delete, so an updated (non-equal) height keeps its original insertion position and does NOT refresh LRU recency; a hot re-measured row can be evicted before genuinely cold entries.

initialMeasurementsCache useMemo depends only on [messagesArray]; a viewport-width change with no message change does not rebuild the seed. Self-protecting (a bucket miss yields no seed) but a missed warm-start after resize.

Both geometry tests await [data-message-id='a-29'] but mixedWindow() builds 60 messages then slice(0,30) keeps only u-0..a-14 — a-29 is absent, so toBeVisible() always times out and both primary perf tests fail.

The warm-reopen test navigates via full page.goto('/') + goto('/chat/:id'); a full document reload wipes the module-level measuredHeightCache (process-lifetime), so initialMeasurementsCache is empty on re-open and the ITEM-2 cache is never exercised — the assertion passes for the wrong reason.

The image is a 240x240 SVG and RESERVED_IMAGE_MIN_HEIGHT is exactly 240, so reserved==natural by construction and abs(afterY-beforeY)<24 is guaranteed regardless of correctness; the residual-jump case (natural!=240) is never tested.

The 'memo boundary' test only asserts errors.toHaveLength(0); it never asserts re-mount/re-highlight behavior, and a blanket toHaveLength(0) on all console errors is flaky against benign unrelated errors. Title overstates what is verified.
Geometry ratio + mounted-count are read after fixed waitForTimeout(600) with no 'measurement settled' signal; on slow CI the virtualizer may not have finished measuring, making the bounds flaky.

The 'policy is UNCHANGED' comments (here + useStreamdownComponents + test header) are inaccurate: the classifier now BLOCKS protocol-relative //host that the old startsWith('/') branch ALLOWED — a real (correct) authz tightening the framing understates.
'lands within a bounded ratio of representative real heights' compares to hardcoded [150,600] bounds with no real measured height — a self-referential sanity bound, not a ratio-to-reality check.

Reviewed error-handling/null-safety across estimateMessageHeight (undefined/empty message), reservedImageBox (partial/non-positive dims), classifyImageSrc (malformed URL try/catch) and the onLoad handler — no unhandled-throw or silent-failure defect found.

Reviewed pure-util + colocated node:test pattern, react-virtual API usage (initialMeasurementsCache/itemSizeCache/getItemKey/getOffsetForIndex), and markdown-override placement against BlockedImage — conforms to surrounding idioms; no anti-pattern found.

Reviewed ReservedImage prop passthrough (src/alt/width/height/onLoad via ...rest), the img override's null-vs-element return contract, and the unchanged MessageListHandle/virtualizer options — contracts honored, no breaking change.

Reviewed ReservedImage a11y — alt forwarded via ...rest, the inline-block wrapper preserves valid

nesting while reserving box height, no focus/scroll-anchor regression.
Reviewed for lost permission/auth checks — none in this diff; the only authz-relevant change is the image-origin policy tightening (recorded under security/i18n-copy), no permission gate removed.
